perturbation.original_text: Representatives ”), each Party agrees to treat the Confidential Information provided to it by the other Party in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement and to take or abstain from taking certain other actions hereinafter set forth. 2. Excluded Information . The Confidential Information shall not include information that (i) is or becomes publicly available other than as a result of acts by the Receiving Party or its Representatives in breach of the terms of this Agreement, (ii) is in the Receiving Party’s possession or the possession of any of its Representatives prior to disclosure by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party or is independently derived by the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives without the aid, application or use of the Confidential Information, (iii) is disclosed to the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives by a third party on a non-confidential basis, or (iv) subject to compliance with paragraph 6 below, the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives is advised by counsel that it is required to be disclosed by applicable law, regulation or legal process.
perturbation.changed_text: Representatives ”), each Party agrees to treat the Confidential Data provided to it by the other Party in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement and to take or abstain from taking certain other actions hereinafter set forth. 2. Excluded Information . The Confidential Information shall not include information that (i) is or becomes publicly available other than as a result of acts by the Receiving Party or its Representatives in breach of the terms of this Agreement, (ii) is in the Receiving Party’s possession or the possession of any of its Representatives prior to disclosure by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party or is independently derived by the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives without the aid, application or use of the Confidential Information, (iii) is disclosed to the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives by a third party on a non-confidential basis, or (iv) subject to compliance with paragraph 6 below, the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives is advised by counsel that it is required to be disclosed by applicable law, regulation or legal process.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Data' in the first sentence of Paragraph 2 creates an internal contradiction. The subsequent sentences in Paragraph 2, and indeed the rest of the document, refer to 'Confidential Information,' leading to uncertainty as to whether the exclusion applies to all information or only some. This is an in-text contradiction because the same paragraph uses both terms without clarifying their relationship.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Excluded Information . The Confidential Information shall not include information that (i) is or becomes publicly available other than as a result of acts by the Receiving Party or its Representatives in breach of the terms of this Agreement, (ii) is in the Receiving Party’s possession or the possession of any of its Representatives prior to disclosure by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party or is independently derived by the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives without the aid, application or use of the Confidential Information, (iii) is disclosed to the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives by a third party on a non-confidential basis, or (iv) subject to compliance with paragraph 6 below, the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives is advised by counsel that it is required to be disclosed by applicable law, regulation or legal process.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Data' in the first sentence creates a contradiction. The 'Contradicted Text' and the subsequent sentences in the Changed Text refer to 'Confidential Information'. This misalignment of terminology creates uncertainty and a direct conflict within the document, because both terms are now used but only one is excluded.

perturbation.original_text: Use and Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information . The Receiving Party and its Representatives shall use the Confidential Information provided to it by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction. The Receiving Party shall keep the Confidential Information provided to it by the Disclosing Party confidential and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever; provided , however , that (i) the Receiving Party may make any disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information provided to it by the Disclosing Party to which the Disclosing Party gives its prior written consent, and (ii) any information contained in the Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party may be disclosed to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who reasonably require access to such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible 1 Transaction and who agree to keep such information confidential. Each Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Use and Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information . The Receiving Party and its Representatives shall use the Confidential Information provided to it by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party solely for the purpose of evaluating a potential Deal. The Receiving Party shall keep the Confidential Information provided to it by the Disclosing Party confidential and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever; provided , however , that (i) the Receiving Party may make any disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information provided to it by the Disclosing Party to which the Disclosing Party gives its prior written consent, and (ii) any information contained in the Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party may be disclosed to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who reasonably require access to such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible 1 Transaction and who agree to keep such information confidential. Each Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'possible Transaction' to 'potential Deal' in the first sentence, while the rest of the paragraph and document continue to use 'Transaction,' introduces an in-text contradiction. It is unclear whether 'potential Deal' is synonymous with 'Transaction'. This change creates uncertainty regarding the scope of the agreement, particularly whether the confidentiality obligations apply equally to both.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Use and Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information . The Receiving Party and its Representatives shall use the Confidential Information provided to it by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party solely for the purpose of evaluating a potential Deal. The Receiving Party shall keep the Confidential Information provided to it by the Disclosing Party confidential and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever; provided , however , that (i) the Receiving Party may make any disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information provided to it by the Disclosing Party to which the Disclosing Party gives its prior written consent, and (ii) any information contained in the Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party may be disclosed to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who reasonably require access to such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible 1 Transaction and who agree to keep such information confidential. Each Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'possible Transaction' with 'potential Deal' in the first sentence, while the contradicted text uses both terms. This introduces misaligned terminology. The explanation highlights that it creates uncertainty whether 'potential Deal' and 'Transaction' are synonymous, leading to conflicting obligations and uncertainty regarding the scope of the agreement. This uncertainty creates an in-text contradiction as per the definition.

perturbation.original_text: Without the prior written consent of the other Party, or except as a Party is advised by counsel is required by applicable law, regulation or legal process, neither Party nor any of its Representatives shall (i) disclose to any other person that it has received Confidential Information from the other Party, or (ii) disclose to any person that discussions or negotiations are taking place between the Parties concerning a possible Transaction, including the status of such discussions or negotiations.
perturbation.changed_text: Without the prior written consent of the other Entity, or except as a Party is advised by counsel is required by applicable law, regulation or legal process, neither Party nor any of its Representatives shall (i) disclose to any other person that it has received Confidential Information from the other Party, or (ii) disclose to any person that discussions or negotiations are taking place between the Parties concerning a possible Transaction, including the status of such discussions or negotiations.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Party' to 'Entity' in the first line creates an ambiguity. While 'Party' is defined in the beginning, 'Entity' is not. This raises questions about whether the consent requirement applies to any 'Entity' associated with the 'Party,' or strictly to the 'Party' itself, especially since the rest of the paragraph refers to 'Party'. This is an in-text contradiction because the first line is using the term 'Entity', which wasn't defined, and in that same paragraph it's being assumed that 'Party' and 'Entity' are one and the same.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure of Existence of Negotiations . Without the prior written consent of the other Entity, or except as a Party is advised by counsel is required by applicable law, regulation or legal process, neither Party nor any of its Representatives shall (i) disclose to any other person that it has received Confidential Information from the other Party, or (ii) disclose to any person that discussions or negotiations are taking place between the Parties concerning a possible Transaction, including the status of such discussions or negotiations.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses the defined term 'Party'. The changed text replaces 'Party' with 'Entity' in the first line of the paragraph. 'Entity' is not defined in the document. The Perturbation Explanation states that this creates ambiguity because the rest of the paragraph continues to use 'Party', creating a contradiction. The Contradicted Text contains the same language as the Changed Text, further cementing the in-text contradiction due to the undefined term 'Entity' being used when 'Party' is intended to be used based on its definition in the original contract.

perturbation.original_text: Promptly upon the written request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall return all copies of the Confidential Information provided by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party to the Disclosing Party, and all other Confidential Information (including, without limitation, all notes, summaries, analysis, compilations, studies, interpretations and memoranda based thereon or extracts, copies and other reproductions thereof or other documents prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect such Confidential Information) shall be destroyed. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party and its Representatives may retain (subject to the terms of this Agreement) copies of the Confidential Information to the extent that such retention is required by applicable law or regulation.
perturbation.changed_text: Promptly upon the written request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall return all copies of the Confidential Material provided by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party to the Disclosing Party, and all other Confidential Information (including, without limitation, all notes, summaries, analysis, compilations, studies, interpretations and memoranda based thereon or extracts, copies and other reproductions thereof or other documents prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect such Confidential Information) shall be destroyed. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party and its Representatives may retain (subject to the terms of this Agreement) copies of the Confidential Information to the extent that such retention is required by applicable law or regulation.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Material' in the first sentence while retaining 'Confidential Information' in the subsequent sentence and throughout the rest of the paragraph, creates an in-text contradiction and uncertainty. It becomes unclear whether the obligation to return extends only to 'Confidential Material' or to all 'Confidential Information' as defined earlier. This is becausethe same paragraph uses both terms without clarifying their relationship.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Confidential Information . Promptly upon the written request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall return all copies of the Confidential Material provided by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party to the Disclosing Party, and all other Confidential Information (including, without limitation, all notes, summaries, analysis, compilations, studies, interpretations and memoranda based thereon or extracts, copies and other reproductions thereof or other documents prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect such Confidential Information) shall be destroyed. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party and its Representatives may retain (subject to the terms of this Agreement) copies of the Confidential Information to the extent that such retention is required by applicable law or regulation.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Material' in the first sentence of the Changed Text, while retaining 'Confidential Information' elsewhere in the paragraph and in the Contradicted Text, creates a clear in-text contradiction. This is because it introduces ambiguity as to whether the return obligation applies only to 'Confidential Material' or to all 'Confidential Information,' as potentially defined earlier. The paragraph in both the Changed and Contradicted texts use both terms, causing uncertainty about their relationship and, therefore, what must be returned. This directly aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction,' where multiple terms are used interchangeably without clear definition, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that a Party or anyone to whom it discloses the Confidential Information provided to it by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party receives a request to disclose all or any part of the Confidential Information provided to it by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party pursuant to applicable law or regulation or under the terms of a subpoena or other order issued by a court of competent jurisdiction or by another governmental agency, such Party shall (provided that such is legally permitted) (i) promptly notify the Disclosing Party of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, (ii) consult with the Disclosing Party on the advisability of taking steps to resist or narrow such request, (iii) if disclosure of such Confidential Information is required, furnish only such portion of the Confidential Information as such Party is advised by counsel is legally required to be disclosed, and (iv) reasonably cooperate with the Disclosing Party in its efforts to obtain a protective order to prevent the Confidential Information from being disclosed or such other order or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be afforded to such portion of the Confidential Information that is required to be disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that a Party or anyone to whom it discloses the Protected Data provided to it by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party receives a request to disclose all or any part of the Confidential Information provided to it by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party pursuant to applicable law or regulation or under the terms of a subpoena or other order issued by a court of competent jurisdiction or by another governmental agency, such Party shall (provided that such is legally permitted) (i) promptly notify the Disclosing Party of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, (ii) consult with the Disclosing Party on the advisability of taking steps to resist or narrow such request, (iii) if disclosure of such Confidential Information is required, furnish only such portion of the Confidential Information as such Party is advised by counsel is legally required to be disclosed, and (iv) reasonably cooperate with the Disclosing Party in its efforts to obtain a protective order to prevent the Confidential Information from being disclosed or such other order or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be afforded to such portion of the Confidential Information that is required to be disclosed.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Data' in the first part of the sentence. Given the rest of the paragraph refers to the term 'Confidential Information', and there is no relationship that establishes what Protected Data and Confidential Information are, it is not clear that Protected Data has the same stipulations. This creates an in-text contradiction because the initial event is triggered when Protected Data is requested to be disclosed, and since Confidential Information is used afterwards, it is no longer clear on which of the two the rest of the paragraph is reffering to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subpoena or Court Order . In the event that a Party or anyone to whom it discloses the Protected Data provided to it by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party receives a request to disclose all or any part of the Confidential Information provided to it by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party pursuant to applicable law or regulation or under the terms of a subpoena or other order issued by a court of competent jurisdiction or by another governmental agency, such Party shall (provided that such is legally permitted) (i) promptly notify the Disclosing Party of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, (ii) consult with the Disclosing Party on the advisability of taking steps to resist or narrow such request, (iii) if disclosure of such Confidential Information is required, furnish only such portion of the Confidential Information as such Party is advised by counsel is legally required to be disclosed, and (iv) reasonably cooperate with the Disclosing Party in its efforts to obtain a protective order to prevent the Confidential Information from being disclosed or such other order or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be afforded to such portion of the Confidential Information that is required to be disclosed.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Data' at the beginning of the sentence in the Changed Text creates a contradiction with the rest of the paragraph, which refers to 'Confidential Information'. The Perturbation Explanation states that without a clear relationship between 'Protected Data' and 'Confidential Information', it's unclear which term the paragraph is referring to after the change. The Contradicted Text is identical to the Changed Text, further emphasizing the contradiction within the same section. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, where using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them leads to conflicting obligations.
